title: Biarum gramineum
taxonomy: Biarum gramineum is classified within the family Colchicaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as autumn crocus or meadow saffron. The Colchicaceae family is part of the larger order Asparagales, which comprises plants with highly modified flowers and a wide range of growth habits. Biarum gramineum is further classified within the genus Biarum, which includes several species of flowering plants found primarily in tropical regions. The specific classification of Biarum gramineum helps to understand its evolutionary history and relationships with other plants.

intro: Biarum gramineum, also known as grass-leaved voodoo lily, is a flowering plant species within the Arum family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. Biarum gramineum contains bioactive constituents including alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties.
